tests.title: Push-to-talk round trip: cues, state machine, spoken reply
tests.requires: App running with mic + speakers; panel open; personality ON.
tests.expected: A held-key utterance flows LISTENINGâ†’TRANSCRIBINGâ†’THINKINGâ†’SPEAKINGâ†’IDLE with the right cue at each stage and a spoken reply that addresses you as "Commander".

steps.text: Hold `[` â†’ you hear a `listen` cue immediately (before speaking).
While holding, say "Hello COVAS, can you hear me? Keep it short." then release â†’ you hear a `processing` cue.
Panel status + log move through LISTENING â†’ TRANSCRIBING â†’ THINKING â†’ SPEAKING â†’ IDLE.
Your words appear as `Commander: â€¦` and the reply as `COVAS: â€¦` (timestamped) in the log.
Just before speech you hear the `completed` cue, then the reply plays in the ElevenLabs voice and addresses you as "Commander".

tests.title: Tap-to-cancel, barge-in, and panel CANCEL all interrupt
tests.requires: App running with mic + speakers; panel open.
tests.expected: A brief tap of `[` (<400 ms) stops thinking/speaking instantly; holding `[` during a reply barges in with a fresh capture; the panel CANCEL button also stops a reply.

steps.text: Ask a long question ("Tell me the history of the Elite Dangerous galaxy in detail."). While it is thinking or speaking, tap `[` briefly â†’ it stops instantly and returns to IDLE.
Confirm a normal hold still records fine (a hold is well over the 400 ms tap threshold).
Barge-in: while a reply is being spoken, hold `[` again â†’ speech cuts off and a fresh capture starts.
The panel's CANCEL / STOP button also stops an in-progress reply.

tests.title: Router picks the cheap tier by default and escalates on demand
tests.requires: `[router].enabled = true`; app running; panel open (read the `[router]` and `[usage]` log lines).
tests.expected: Each turn logs a `[router] [<tier>] <model> max_tokens=N â€” <reason>` line (the tier prefix is from issue #11) and a `[usage] â€¦ [<model>]` line; banter uses [cheap] Haiku, depth/"think hard" escalates to [standard] Sonnet, explicit "use Opus" uses [premium] Opus, and "full breakdown" raises max_tokens.

steps.text: Banter cheap tier: "Morning, COVAS â€” how's it going?" â†’ router line shows `[cheap] claude-haiku-4-5`; cost a fraction of a cent.
"Think hard about the best way to break in a new ship." â†’ `[standard] claude-sonnet-5`.
Depth phrase: "Walk me through the pros and cons of a fuel scoop." â†’ [standard] Sonnet.
Explicit premium: "Use Opus for this â€” summarize the Thargoid war." â†’ `[premium] claude-opus-4-8`.
"Give me the full breakdown of the engineering process." â†’ higher `max_tokens` (2048).
(Optional) Set the router pin in Settings (`cheap`/`standard`/`premium`, or `haiku`/`sonnet`/`opus`) and confirm the router line's tier + model reflect it.

tests.title: Automatic web search on current-info questions, capped and cancelable
tests.requires: `[web_search].enabled = true`; app running; panel open; internet.
tests.expected: A current-info question triggers a logged web search (with a processing beep), the answer reflects live info, mid-search tap-cancel stops it, and searches are capped at `max_uses` (3) per reply.

steps.text: "What's the latest Elite Dangerous update right now?" â†’ log shows "Searching the web for â€¦", status hits a searching state, you hear a processing beep.
The spoken answer reflects live/current info.
Cancel mid-search: start another current-info question, then tap `[` while searching â†’ it stops.
Searches are capped at `[web_search].max_uses` (3) per reply.

tests.title: Odd input, session log, provider hiccup, clean quit
tests.expected: Emoji/symbol input streams without crashing; the newest `logs\session_*.log` holds timestamped prompts/replies + router/usage; a brief network kill degrades gracefully back to IDLE (dead TTS falls back to text); Ctrl+Alt+Q shuts down cleanly.

steps.text: Ask for something with odd symbols/emoji ("Draw me an ASCII arrow and explain it.") â†’ speaks/streams without the console crashing.
Open the newest `logs\session_*.log` â†’ prompts + replies with timestamps, plus router/usage lines.
A provider hiccup (briefly kill network) degrades gracefully â€” the loop survives and returns to IDLE; a dead TTS falls back to text.
Ctrl+Alt+Q (or closing the console window) shuts it down cleanly.
